desc: Community Health Workforce Professionalisation and Career Pathway Development Support
full: Malawi’s community health workforce including HSAs, CHNs, and CHVs forms the backbone of primary health care delivery, particularly at the community level. Despite their critical role, the workforce faces fragmented career pathways, limited progression opportunities, and inconsistent integration into formal health structures. Strengthening the professionalisation of this cadre is essential to improve motivation, retention, and quality of service delivery, in line with HSSP III, the National Community Health Framework, and broader HRH reforms.To address these gaps, UNICEF Malawi requests technical assistance to professionalise the community health workforce, with a primary focus on designing clear career progression pathways. The support will define competency standards, accreditation, and progression for all community cadres, integrate them into formal PHC structures with appropriate supervision and retention mechanisms, ensure alignment with district operational plans and national HRH systems, and provide guidance for institutionalisation and sustainable workforce management to strengthen PHC delivery and advance Universal Health Coverage.

desc: TA in Strategic Moment of reflection for the Mid point review of the CP
full: The mission was undertaken to support UNICEF Malawi during the Mid-Point Review (MPR) of the Country Programme, with a specific focus on Outcome 1 (Health, HIV, Nutrition and WASH). The objective was to participate in the Strategic Moment of Reflection (SMR), assess progress toward SDG targets, identify binding constraints to child survival, and provide strategic guidance aligned with the UNICEF Strategic Plan (SP) 2026–2029

desc: Resilient PHC Systems and Emergency Preparedness Support
full: Malawi’s primary health care system is increasingly exposed to multiple emergency hazards, including climate shocks, disease outbreaks, and other public health threats, which threaten service continuity and resilience. Despite progress in PHC coverage, emergency events can rapidly disrupt essential services, particularly for vulnerable populations. Strengthening system resilience and maintaining or increasing PHC intervention coverage requires strategic approaches that integrate preparedness, adaptive service delivery, and coordinated response mechanisms, in line with HSSP III and broader health sector reforms.To address these challenges, UNICEF Malawi requests technical assistance to support the strategic design and implementation of PHC interventions that maintain and expand coverage while enhancing system resilience. The support will focus on adaptive service delivery models, strategic prepositioning of essential supplies and resources, and the development of expanded multi-stakeholder partnerships to ensure continuity of services during emergencies. The TA will provide guidance on operationalising emergency preparedness and response plans within routine PHC delivery, identifying system bottlenecks, optimizing resource allocation, and integrating lessons from previous hazard responses to strengthen both short-term emergency response and long-term system resilience.Expected outputs include a strategic framework for resilient PHC delivery under emergency conditions, operational guidance for adaptive service delivery, prepositioning plans for essential supplies, and partnership strategies to support sustainable emergency response and continuity of essential PHC services.

desc: Shift Eritrea’s food systems from production-focused approaches to nutrition outcomes through integrated value chains, schools, food safety, and SBC
full:
Module A — Rapid food systems mapping & priority entry points (≈ 8 weeks) 

What CoE does: Produce a concise map of nutrition-sensitive value chains (e.g., cereals/legumes, dairy) and school-based entry points (gardens/menus), with a baseline of risks and simple indicators. Identify quick wins in food safety and hygiene practices across small processors/school kitchens; map governance and cross-ministry roles (MoA/MoH/MoE/MLWE). 

What it strengthens: Shared picture of “where to start” linking production to nutrition outcomes; early list of feasible pilots; clarity on roles and decision points. 

Standards/tools: UNICEF food systems/nutrition guidance; simple value-chain and risk screening templates; school nutrition checklist. 

Module B — Complementary foods mini-pilot (DMK+) & micro-processing support 

What CoE does: Co-design a small DMK+ complementary food mini-pilot: product specifications, quality/batch SOPs, basic HACCP-inspired hygiene steps, packaging/labelling, and micro-processing coaching for 2–3 local producer units (or school kitchens). Develop simple QA tools and a child-feeding counselling card that links to the product. 

What it strengthens: Safe, acceptable complementary foods with clear quality steps; local capacity to produce and coach caregivers; documented micro-processing SOPs for replication. 

Standards/tools: UNICEF IYCF resources; QA/food safety lite templates; behaviour change job-aids. 

Module C — Food safety & fortification “lite”: SOPs and training 

What CoE does: Draft light SOPs for hygiene, storage, and fortification/processing suitable for small establishments and schools; run two short training cycles (virtual + in-country practical sessions) for MoH/MoA/MoE/MMR/MTI teams and local producers. Provide model procurement specs (e.g., packaging, basic test kits) and simple monitoring forms. 

What it strengthens: Practical, context-appropriate standards that reduce contamination and variability; entry-level capacity to monitor compliance. 

Standards/tools: Food safety checklists; school kitchen hygiene SOP; fortification basics; simple monitoring templates. 

Module D — Healthy diets SBC & school-based pilots (gardens/menus/peer clubs) 

What CoE does: Package an SBC toolkit (scripts/posters/mini-sessions) on diverse diets and safe food handling; set up 10–15 school garden/menu/peer clubs pilots with seasonally feasible crops and a simple menu planning sheet; define a light results log (attendance, menu diversity, hygiene practices). 

What it strengthens: Community and school behaviours for healthier diets; practical experience with school gardens and simple menu standards; early evidence for scale-up. 

Standards/tools: SBC toolkit; garden starter kit; menu diversity scorecard; hygiene reminders.

desc: Generate evidence to guide nutrition actions for pregnant women and children in conflict-affected Abkhazia region of Georgia
full:
This initiative represents an unprecedented effort in Abkhazia region of Georgia, which remains outside government control and is characterized by a highly sensitive political context. The intervention aims to assess health, nutrition, and overall well-being among vulnerable populations, specifically:
• Pregnant women
• Children under 5 years old
• School-aged children (7–9 years old)
The primary goal is to generate evidence-based insights that will inform the development of targeted nutrition actions for these groups. This approach is critical for addressing gaps in health and nutrition services in a protracted conflict setting.

It will also support the development of a knowledge-generation agenda aligned with India’s priorities. Additionally, the assistance will strengthen climate-smart nutrition strategies and innovative financing models, including leveraging climate funds and the Child Nutrition Fund. This support aims to position nutrition as a core priority in the 2028–2032 India Country Programme while developing an adapted set of indicators to measure UNICEF’s contribution to India’s Nutrition Agenda.

desc: Prevent post-cyclone nutrition and food security crises through anticipatory, child-focused action
full: UNICEF anticipates heightened food insecurity in Q2–Q3 2026 as the cumulative effects of Cyclone Ditwah, high food prices, and already elevated child malnutrition converge. Both wasting and stunting rates were concerning prior to the cyclone, and we expect additional shocks to push vulnerable households into deeper food stress. Our priority for 2026 is to position UNICEF to lead the development of a coherent package of short-term and medium-term interventions—ranging from targeted nutrition support and continuity of essential services to systems-level measures that strengthen surveillance, early warning, and household resilience. Sri Lanka will have limited fiscal space next year (and the CO will have limited funding) but the need for clear analysis, costed options, and strong advocacy remains urgent. UNICEF will need to identify feasible entry points and shaping an integrated proposal/clear advocacy that government and partners can get behind, rollout and budget for in subsequent years.

desc: Establish a national nutrition financing platform to mobilize, align, and sustain investments for child nutrition in Guinea-Bissau
full:
Technical Assistance for a National Nutrition Financing Platform
Guinea-Bissau’s nutrition sector remains critically underfunded by both development partners and the government, despite its importance for child survival and growth. Designing and operationalizing a national nutrition financing platform will mobilize, coordinate, and align resources to scale up child nutrition interventions. This platform will also leverage existing and new funding streams to ensure sustainable impact. Targeted investment in nutrition can deliver transformative results for children, contributing to global goals of ensuring millions of children are well-nourished and able to thrive.

Assist in designing national immunization strategies, including new vaccine introduction and reaching zero-dose children. Support development of national strategies for scaling up neonatal and community health and mobilizing resources. Contribute to the Water and Sanitation strategic plan under the NDP “Tchad Connexion 2030.” Strengthen equity and demand through SBC, WASH, social marketing, and community engagement. Build partnerships with small-scale nutritious food producers and women’s cooperatives for sustainable scale-up.

desc: Accelerate Niger’s nutrition system transformation by integrating wasting treatment, first foods, prevention, and CNF governance
full: NUTRITION: The technical assistance will provide expert guidance to implement nutrition policies, mobilize public and private financing, and draft legislation that strengthens governance for nutrition. It will support the design of scalable, integrated programs for prevention of child wasting, maternal nutrition, and improving access to safe, diverse first foods. Additionally, the assistance will develop a knowledge-generation agenda aligned with Niger’s priorities to inform evidence-based decision-making. It will also reinforce national nutrition strategies and innovative financing, leveraging mechanisms such as the Child Nutrition Fund to ensure sustainable interventions across health, food, education, and social protection systems.

desc: Strengthen DRC’s IMAM system to deliver accountable, protocol-compliant treatment with protected RUTF supply chains

desc: Review the CODE Law and subsidiary regulations to strengthen implementation, enforcement, and advocacy mechanisms.
full: Technical assistance to review the CODE Law and its subsidiary regulations, with a focus on strengthening implementation, enforcement, and advocacy mechanisms. This will include an assessment of legal and institutional arrangements, enforcement processes and capacities, monitoring and reporting systems, and advocacy approaches to address gaps in compliance. The review will generate actionable recommendations to enhance alignment with international standards, strengthen accountability across sectors, and support effective protection of infant and young child feeding practices within broader food‑environment and nutrition frameworks.

desc: Risk mapping and mitigation plan development for the government free meals programme serving under-fives and school-age children.
full: Risk mapping and development of a mitigation plan for the free meals programme.                                                                                               The Government of Indonesia has made a significant investment to improve nutrition outcomes through provision of free nutritious meals to Under five children, Pregnant and breastfeeding women and school age children. As the programme is being rolled out, several challenges such as food poisoning have emerged. UNICEF has been supporting the Government to establish the center of Excellence (CoE) for the free meal programme. it was requested to develop a risk mapping and mitigation plan to address the issues that might hinder the programme of achieving its expected results. As the main partner of the CoE, UNICEF is seeking technical assistance to carry out risk mapping and develop a strategic action plan to address and mitigate the impact of these risks. ( risk mapping exercise and mitiagation measures to be implemented)

desc: Technical and policy assistance to update Bolivia's Law on Healthy Eating including labelling regulations and advocacy for Congressional approval.
full: Currently, Bolivia’s Law No. 775 on Healthy Eating needs to be updated to include adjustments to food labeling and related sanctions. To date, this law lacks implementing regulations, making it urgent to provide technical and policy assistance not only for updating the law and drafting its regulations but also for securing approval in the National Congress. It is essential to define an advocacy/policy strategy based on evidence (including South-South Cooperation) to facilitate approval of the updated law and its regulations. Passing this law will enable the implementation of strategies to prevent overweight and obesity among children and adolescents.

desc: Routine TA to strengthen ECD implementation and malnutrition prevention through an integrated intersectoral approach.
full: Routine technical assistance to strengthen Early Childhood Development (ECD) implementation and malnutrition prevention through an integrated, intersectoral approach combining WASH, health, nutrition, education and public policy. Support is needed to ensure technical coherence and operational alignment across sectors, including harmonized strategies, priority interventions and messaging, and improved coordination for implementation in prioritized territories. This request will contribute to stronger prevention-oriented programming, improved service delivery quality, and more effective systems strengthening to reduce stunting and other forms of child malnutrition, while advancing holistic outcomes for young children.

desc: Routine technical support for day-to-day IMAM and PPCMA implementation, supply-chain integrity, and financing readiness.
full: Routine technical assistance is required to support day-to-day implementation of IMAM/PPCMA programmes, with a focus on operational compliance, supply-chain integrity, and financing readiness. This includes regular technical review of IMAM/PPCMA implementation reports, troubleshooting protocol deviations, routine analysis of RUTF pipeline and consumption data, validation of traceability and fraud-risk flags, and hands-on support to country teams in preparing briefs, advocacy notes, and funding justifications for RUTF procurement. The assistance will ensure timely problem-solving, data-informed decisions, and continuous alignment with WHO 2023 guidance

desc: Technical assistance to develop a multi-year Child Nutrition Fund proposal with quality assurance and costing support.
full: Short-term, periodic Center of Excellence support is required to provide practical, hands-on technical assistance that support the development and preparation of a multi-year Child Nutrition Fund proposal. This support will include targeted technical feedbacks, quality assurance of the prepared document. But also support the development of the planning, costing, evidence use to finalize the proposal. The assistance will facilitate coordination and validation with national authorities, ensure alignment with Child Nutrition Fund match and programmatic window requirements, and maintain implementation momentum. By 2026, this time-bound support will enable development, government approval, and submission of a coherent, high-quality multi-year proposal.

desc: CCO orientation on OPIC
full: The Cambodia Country Office is planning a learning series for programme staff on OPIC (CRC optional protocol n. 3). We are seeking to get support from a colleague from the human rights division to deliver an online session covering what the protocol entails, its relevance for child rights, and the importance of ratification, etc.  We are planning for a session of approximately 75–90 minutes, including  Q&A and reflections from colleagues at the CCO. We would tentatively hold this session on 25 or 26 of May starting at 1:30 pm local time in Cambodia. Alternatively, we would also consider the week of the 8th of June., depending on colleagues' availability. Attaching the concept note of the learning series for further information.

full: Technical assistance to strengthen the governance structure of the Shabab Balad board and its coalitions to ensure clear roles, accountability, and effective coordination across government, private sector, and UN partners. The assistance will also help develop a Monitoring and Evaluation framework with measurable indicators, clearly defined outcome pathways, and practical mechanisms to track progress and impact at scale. In addition, the TA will support the design of an integrated, government-led end-to-end pathway that links skills development with employability and entrepreneurship opportunities—including emerging sectors such as health, digital, green—ensuring that every young person—particularly girls, adolescents with disabilities, refugees, and those from underserved communities—can equitably and safely transition from learning to dignified and sustainable livelihoods.

desc: Scale Generation Unlimited Ecuador as a multi-sector platform for youth skills, jobs, participation, and protection
full: Technical assistance is required to consolidate GenU governance and operating model, develop a clear 2026–2029 roadmap, strengthen the programme package (skills, employability, partnerships), and establish a robust results framework and monitoring approach. Support is needed to strengthen inter-ministerial coordination mechanisms, define scalable models and implementation pathways, and leverage private-sector engagement. Assistance is also requested to develop a resource mobilization strategy linked to measurable outcomes (including innovative financing options), and to capture and adapt regional best practices to accelerate implementation and scale-up.

desc: Establish Generation Unlimited Colombia as a national platform linking education, skills, jobs, and youth participation
full: "Technical assistance to the Country Office on Generation Unlimited (GenU) should focus on: Strategy: clarifying GenU’s value proposition within the country programme, aligning it with national youth, education, employment, peacebuilding, and private-sector engagement priorities, and defining measurable system-level outcomes. Sequencing: supporting phased implementation—from ecosystem mapping and pilots to scale-up—ensuring coherence across sectors and avoiding fragmentation. Partnerships: identifying, structuring, and managing high-impact partnerships with government, private sector, civil society, and youth-led organizations, including clear roles and co-investment models. Strategic engagement: strengthening senior-level engagement with key partners to position GenU as a shared platform for systemic change rather than a stand-alone project."

desc: Review and strengthen cluster coordination to align with the Global Humanitarian Reset
full: "Malawi’s humanitarian coordination is at a crossroads, with the global reset calling for simplified, decentralized structures and stronger country-level leadership. Currently, 11 clusters operate under DoDM. Malawi faces donor concerns about weak coordination and coherence. UNICEF co-leads several clusters and is actively supporting the transition, yet there is a clear need for leaner, more inclusive models. To address these challenges, we requests technical support from EOE for an inter-cluster coordination review and cluster simplification. The goal is to align with global recommendations, reducing and merging clusters, adopting area-based coordination, and establishing minimum structures while ensuring local leadership and inclusion."

desc: Strengthen the humanitarian-development nexus to optimize outcomes for children
full: Technical assistance on the humanitarian-development nexus will support the country office to optimally adapt its programming to the different crisis situations in the country whilst optimizing further the opportunities that programming along the nexus provides.

desc: Humanitarian Reset and FFI implications in a protracted crisis context Accompany/ rapid review of how clusters in reforming how they operate to better...
full: "Humanitarian Reset and FFI implications in a protracted crisis context Accompany/ rapid review of how clusters in reforming how they operate to better address the complex, overlapping crises while strengthening local humanitarian leadership capacity."

desc: Advance digital transformation to strengthen systems, service delivery, and results at scale
full: Technical assistance is required to support the design and phased implementation of an interoperable digital ecosystem for child-related services, linking sectoral information systems with planning, monitoring, and decision-support tools. Support will focus on digital architecture, data governance, interoperability standards, capacity development, and change management, ensuring alignment with national digital strategies and scalable, government-owned digital public infrastructure.

desc: SCO seeks targeted, routine technical assistance to strengthen multi-sectoral systems, accelerate the scale-up of proven and innovative approaches, and...

desc: Enhance water security for last-mile Afghan households through climate-adaptive, snowmelt-based solutions
full:
Technical assistance to design climate-resilient water security solutions for vulnerable households in hard-to-reach mountainous and snow-covered areas of Afghanistan in the context of a rapidly changing climate context. This includes multidisciplinary expertise in climate scenario development, hydrogeology, water recharge systems modelling, and innovative technologies and indigenous knowledge for rain and snowmelt harvesting for water security. 
  
Technical assistance will lead to a strategy that will guide interventions, measure water access outcomes, strengthen evidence-based programming at scale, ensuring sustainability, resilience, and reduced vulnerability to climate variability for the most affected and marginalized communities.

This strategic restructuring challenges the Bolivia Country Office to establish stronger synergies among thematic sectors such as WASH, health, nutrition, and early childhood. The technical assistance required is aimed at supporting the sectoral integration process and identifying key actions to effectively implement the holistic approach of Goal Area/Impact 1.

desc: Develop a WASH costing and financing strategy by mapping needs, analysing funding options, and reviewing the national WASH assessment.
full:
WASH costing & WASH Financing strategy development
Lead a sector WASH financing strategy: map needs/costs by sub‑sector (rural/urban water, sanitation, hygiene), analyse funding/financing options (treasury, tariffs, transfers, blended finance), and propose concrete measures and governance for implementation—per UNICEF global guidance. updating the national One WASH Strategy and Investment Plan developed in 2019, drawing on evidence from the recently completed National WASH Assessment covering rural and urban water supply, sanitation, WASH in schools, and WASH in health care facilities. The support will include a systematic mapping of updated needs and costs across all WASH sub-sectors, reflecting current service gaps, climate-related pressures, and equity and sustainability considerations

Review of WASH assessment report
Provide an independent technical review of the WASH assessment: validate methods and findings, identify data gaps, and deliver actionable recommendations on service models, investment priorities, and risk mitigation, feeding into the financing strategy and urban plans. It will also analyse available and potential financing and funding options—including government budget allocations, tariffs, transfers, donor funding, opportunities for blended finance, and contributions from Eritrean diaspora communities—assessing their feasibility, predictability, and sustainability within the Eritrean context.

Technical support on Urban WASH
Provide urban WASH systems support: service planning, utility strengthening, climate risk screening, inclusive service models, asset management and performance monitoring.

desc: Conceptualise/outline a ODF Gameplan
full:
Priority#2:
TA support to conceptualise/outline a ODF Gameplan (with practical strategies to achieve and sustain ODF-free status) and to connect to COs who have this experience 
Currently: ODF rates in The Gambia are about 1% but they have been at this rate for many years. High level of political engagement to achieve ODF-free status. Lots of strong political will. Govt have WB funds that can support the 'last push' for the last mile but need guidance on the 'how' - hence the need for Gameplan . Mapping of the areas of highest burden underway. 
Govt has a 5 year strategy ending in 2027 and would like to achieve ODF-status by end of 2027
Gameplan should include incentives and opportunities to mobilise communities (can build on Armand's experience from Niger on collective movement ewas established and how private sector wa mobilised)

desc: Scale household access to safe water and sanitation in vulnerable regions of Mexico
full: Under the forthcoming CPD, WASH is integrated into the health/nutrition and education outcomes. For health and nutrition, WASH contributes by improving access to safe water and sanitation in remote and rural areas, where service gaps remain a key driver of child undernutrition. As Mexico is a high middle-income country, the UNICEF Country Office (CO) will not directly deliver community-level WASH services, but will focus on influencing sub-national government planning, financing, and investments. This represents a new workstream for the CO, which has previously focused on WASH in schools and emergency response.

desc: Help municipalities design equity-focused WASH programmes for vulnerable groups, sustaining ODF status and improving hygiene outcomes.
full: Routine TA is needed to help municipalities design and implement equity-focused WASH programmes that target vulnerable groups, sustain ODF status, and improve hygiene outcomes. TA will support disability-inclusive WASH designs, MHM-friendly institutional WASH models, and localized interventions. It will also help Palikas establish equity-focused targeting, monitoring, and outreach mechanisms to reduce slippage and improve safely managed sanitation outcomes.

desc: Strengthen NWASH implementation, digital data quality, and integration of WASH indicators into HMIS, EMIS, and SORMAS.
full: TA is required to strengthen NWASH implementation, improve digital data quality, and integrate WASH indicators into HMIS, EMIS, and SORMAS. Municipalities also need guidance on using GIS-based asset registries, digital water-quality reporting, and digital O&M tools. The TA will build subnational capacity to analyze data, generate dashboards, and use evidence for regulation, planning, and performance management across the WASH sector.

desc: Integrate climate adaptation, mitigation, DRR, and community resilience into national WASH frameworks including the SDP and NDC 3.0.
full: Climate-Resilient WASH Systems Strengthening across National Frameworks: To achieve this, Nepal requires sustained technical assistance to fully integrate climate adaptation, mitigation, disaster risk reduction and community resilience into national WASH frameworks, including the WASH Sector Development Plan (SDP), the NDC 3.0 implementation plan, and the WASH Financing Strategy. This support should include developing robust analytical inputs that align sector indicators with national climate commitments and strengthening cross-sectoral coordination mechanisms to ensure coherence between WASH, health, environment, and disaster management systems.

desc: Support municipal WASH budgeting, tariff-setting, pro-poor financing, and integration of climate-resilient WASH costs into budgets.
full: Municipalities face challenges in budgeting, lifecycle costing, and securing predictable financing for WASH systems. Technical assistance is needed to support the development of municipal budgeting protocols, tariff-setting guidance, pro-poor financing mechanisms, and the integration of climate-resilient WASH costs into annual and medium-term budgeting processes. This includes coaching Palikas on financial modelling, O&M financing, and identifying opportunities to leverage blended finance, PPPs, and climate-finance instruments.

desc: Mobilize climate finance and develop a National WASH Climate Financing Pipeline aligned with GCF, Adaptation Fund, and blended finance.
full: Climate Finance Mobilization and Development of a National WASH Climate Financing Pipeline: Nepal faces urgent and growing needs for climate-resilient WASH infrastructure and service funding, yet national technical capacity to mobilize climate finance remains limited. Targeted technical assistance is required to identify financing opportunities, structure a national WASH climate-financing pipeline, and support the preparation of proposals aligned with the Green Climate Fund, the Adaptation Fund, and blended financing mechanisms. This includes developing strong technical justifications, people-centered and community-cosmovision-oriented resilience investment cases, and options for leveraging private-sector participation.

desc: Strengthen government and PCO capacity on financially viable WASH service delivery models through technical support and South-South learning.
full: Strengthen government and PCO capacity on financially viable WASH service delivery models, identifying strategic entry points for UNICEF through technical support and South-South learning/cooperation

desc: Strengthen the WASH workforce through competency-based training and support climate-responsive programming with integrated guidelines.
full: Technical assistance is required to strengthen the WASH workforce through institutionalized, competency-based training aligned with national systems. In parallel, support is needed to build sector-wide understanding and implementation of climate-responsive programming by developing climate-integrated guidelines and delivering targeted training that enables effective multi-sector coordination.

desc: Build climate-resilient WASH systems by integrating CEED priorities in São Tomé and Príncipe
full: Technical assistance is required to design integrated WASH–CEED frameworks, strengthen institutional capacity, and mobilize resources for climate-resilient service delivery. Support will include developing national guidelines, piloting scalable interventions, and embedding monitoring mechanisms to track climate and WASH outcomes. Assistance will also focus on fostering cross-sectoral coordination, building workforce skills, and ensuring sustainability of infrastructure and services. This will enable government and partners to deliver holistic interventions that address both immediate WASH needs and long-term climate resilience, ensuring children grow in safe, sustainable, and climate-adapted environments.

desc: Technical support to develop a Climate Resilient WASH financing strategy including a costed investment needs and financing gap analysis.

UNICEF Niger will require technical assistance for the development of the Climate Resilient WASH financing strategy for Niger.

UNICEF Niger will recruit a consultant for this assignment. The role of the CoE will be to provide  technical support and guidance in the process of  developping the WASH Financing Strategy . In this process, a costed investment needs and financing gap analysis will be conducted and a sustainable WASH financing Framework will be developed and this will include financing implementation plan.

desc: Enhance water security for last-mile Afghan households through climate-adaptive, snowmelt-based solutions
full:
Technical assistance to design climate-resilient water security solutions for vulnerable households in hard-to-reach mountainous and snow-covered areas of Afghanistan in the context of a rapidly changing climate context. This includes multidisciplinary expertise in climate scenario development, hydrogeology, water recharge systems modelling, and innovative technologies and indigenous knowledge for rain and snowmelt harvesting for water security.
 
Technical assistance will lead to a strategy that will guide interventions, measure water access outcomes, strengthen evidence-based programming at scale, ensuring sustainability, resilience, and reduced vulnerability to climate variability for the most affected and marginalized communities.

desc: Yemen is conducting Groundwater potential mapping and would like oversight support and insights from lessons learnt in other countries
full:
The Minister of Water has requested UNICEF to develop an Integrated Watershed Management Plan for Taizz City. This task was broken down into 3 studies (i) a remote sending study to develop a groundwater potential map, (ii) an emergency well field and water supply infrastructure study, and (iii) the development of an Integrated Watershed Management Plan, building on the first 2 studies.
Under the UNICEF ToR for the Remote Sensing study, the primary scope was focused on remote sensing–based groundwater potential mapping. In a first step Acacia Water conducted a data gap analysis, including: (i) a clear overview of datasets to be used, (ii) verification of required data availability and quality, (iii) a structured data gap analysis, and (iv) recommendation for the way forward, was conducted through an existing standby agreement mechanism. The intention was to keep the assignment of the actual Remote Sensing study targeted, realistic and cost-efficient, with additional analytical layers potentially integrated in the actual Remote Sensing study.
The Ministry of Water has since proposed expanding the scope of the Remote Sensing study to include additional components: agricultural water demand assessment, surface runoff evaluation, land-use analysis and its impact on water use, climate change considerations, incorporation of existing groundwater monitoring data, and integration of water quality datasets. Given this expanded scope, we request: (1) an indication on associated costs for expanded scope; (2) Support with the technical evaluation of the proposals expected by the end of March, (3) sharing lessons learned and cost benchmarks from similar groundwater mapping initiatives in other country contexts to inform decision-making (4) Oversight support during implementation.

To ensure this platform is sustainably institutionalized into the DWA's  systems including developing a Standard (SOPs), we require specialized support.

This support will enable the Ministry of Health and partners to transition from paper‑based data collection to a digital, real‑time system that ensures timely analysis, transparency, and responsiveness. The assistance will focus on:

Designing and deploying digital tools for feedback collection, reporting, and analysis.

Building partner capacities to manage and sustain the digital system.

Establishing protocols for documentation and learning, ensuring evidence informs program adaptation and scale‑up.

By modernizing the feedback mechanism, technical assistance will strengthen institutional accountability, community trust, and the overall effectiveness of health interventions.

desc: Please add a short description for this TA Request with not more then as 160 characters including space.
full:
6. Child Rights Advocacy: Safeguarding Legal Standards and Policy Commitments for Adolescents

Technical support is required to provide high-level advocacy and legal review to safeguard child rights amid policy backsliding risks. This includes reviewing draft laws and policy proposals, preparing advocacy briefs, and providing technical advice on international standards related to minimum age of criminal responsibility and adolescent rights. Assistance will also involve developing evidence-based arguments, troubleshooting legislative gaps, and supporting UNICEF’s engagement with government and civil society to prevent harmful measures such as lowering the age of criminal responsibility or creating isolated facilities for adolescents. These targeted inputs will sustain ongoing advocacy and protect child rights commitments.

desc: Support with roll out of the Nadiif Integrated Model for rural sanitation in Somalia
full:
UNICEF Somalia will roll out its Nadiif Integrated Model for rural sanitation starting pilot interventions in selected districts. The Model proposes three interconnected pillars on strengthening government leadership, an enhanced approach to CLTS and harnessing the private sector.
Lack of government oversight on sanitation and in-access to supplies have been a major constraint.
As part of institutional preparation and readiness for this roll out, UNICEF is supporting the government to put in place statutory instruments to guide this effort.
Somalia country office requests support and inputs to the development of sanitation guidelines and standards, regulatory framework for sanitation, design of the PPP strategy for sanitation, & policy review?

desc: Support development of a national WASH Financing Strategy for Somalia.
full:
Somalia is one of the ASWA III countries. Under this programme, the Dutch government prioritized a few thematic areas including WASH financing.  UNICEF Somalia is undertaking analysis in this area to obtain data and information to inform development of a WASH Financing Strategy. The aim is to develop a holistic WASH Sector Financing Strategy that aligns with Somalia’s WASH national policy commitments, National Transformation Plan and/or Sustainable Development Goal 6 (SDG 6) aspirations.
For this, UNICEF Somalia requests technical support from refining data collection tools to review of the report/ findings and the development of a WASH Financing Strategy.

desc: ASWA: Support towards Gender Landscape Analysis in Puntland and Somaliland.
full: Under ASWA III, the request is for technical support to conduct a gender landscape analysis in Somalia. The analysis aims to identify gender related gaps, power dynamics, inequalities, barriers, opportunities, and systemic issues affecting WASH access, governance, participation, and outcomes. It will generate practical, evidence based recommendations and tools to strengthen gender transformative and inclusive WASH programming across the programme cycle. The required support will include technical inputs throughout the gender landscape analysis process, including preparatory activities such as partner capacity building and training prior to data collection, review of the draft report following completion of the analysis, development of an action and response plan, and preparation of training and capacity building materials.

desc: Support development and review of climate rationales for Somaliland and Puntland.
full:
Development of a climate rationale is one of the key priorities under ASWA III.
The process is already underway in both states, with Somaliland having completed the climate risk analysis and solutions workshop to identify feasible measures to address the identified risks.
The climate rationales will support:
• Policy and planning processes
• Design and targeting of climate resilient WASH programmes
• Development of climate finance proposals, including for the Green Climate Fund
• Improved alignment between WASH and climate priorities, including Somalia’s updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) and the National Adaptation Plan (NAP)

The required support includes technical review of the climate risk and solutions reports generated through these processes, as well as review of the final Climate Rationale products for Somaliland and Puntland.

desc: Support for WASH-focused conflict and peace analysis.
full:
WASH Conflict Analysis is a key priority under ASWA III.
The purpose of the WASH Conflict Analysis is to develop and implement a WASH focused Conflict and Peace Analysis (CPA) that integrates gender equality, disability, and social inclusion considerations, and to use the findings to strengthen the integration of conflict sensitivity and peacebuilding into WASH programming. The participatory conflict analysis will support identification and addressing of the root causes of WASH related conflict in Puntland and Somaliland, while targeted capacity development support will strengthen the capacity of UNICEF staff and sector partners.
The required support to the Somalia Country Office includes review of the WASH Conflict Analysis report and development of training materials to support capacity building of staff and partners for effective integration of the findings into conflict sensitive and peacebuilding focused WASH programming.

desc: Support documentation of Urban WASH results to strengthen advocacy, visibility, and partner engagement.
full:
UNICEF Somalia is implementing a broad portfolio of Urban WASH interventions, including strengthening water utilities, developing infrastructure, reducing non-revenue water, and improving sanitation services in major urban centers. keeping in view the high impact work, there is requirement of structured documentation capturing results, lessons learned, and overall impact.
The Country Office is seeking support from the Centre of Excellence (CoE) in Nairobi to develop a comprehensive suite of communication and knowledge products. This will include success stories, human-interest narratives, technical case studies, before-and-after analyses, and donor-ready publications. 
This initiative will help position Urban WASH as a flagship programme, strengthen advocacy efforts, and enhance engagement with government counterparts and development partners.

full: Collate and synthesise existing hydrogeological and groundwater information into a structured groundwater atlas and spatial files. Work includes establishing and managing the sub-agreement with the British Geological Survey (BGS), digitising existing maps, and scanning/organising relevant historical reports and map resources so they can be shared and used by sector partners. Current progress includes map digitisation underway and a draft atlas being developed.

desc: 【UTS】Life-cycle costing for climate-resilient rural sanitation (3 provinces)
full: Apply a life-cycle costing approach to estimate the full cost of moving rural households from open defecation to basic sanitation and to climate-resilient sanitation across three provinces. The work combines data analysis with statistical/spatial methods to extrapolate costs at provincial level, building on existing data and design efforts for the Government’s No Open Defecation (NOD) programme. Outputs are being shaped through stakeholder engagement, including a government/stakeholder workshop held in Honiara (24 March 2026) and dissemination moments such as sector events

desc: 【UTS】NbS monitoring system for water availability & resilience
full: Design a monitoring approach (indicators, tools, and methods) to assess how nature-based water conservation interventions contribute to water availability and selected dimensions of community resilience. The approach builds on the regional resilience monitoring guidance and is intended for piloting and potential adoption within wider sector monitoring led by DNRAS and partners, including UNICEF’s collaboration with Permatil. Progress includes agreement on the monitoring approach, training delivered with Permatil, researchers recruited for the community-resilience component, and field equipment delivered (loggers/flow-measurement tools).

desc: 【UTS】Coastal salinity testing & analysis for climate response
full: Support salinity monitoring in selected coastal locations through government-led surveys and technical review, focusing on conductivity and TDS indicators to identify potential salinity intrusion risks. Initial surveys in three locations were conducted and reviewed, indicating salinity did not appear excessive in those sites; next steps include UNICEF-led quality assurance/validation and additional technical review by UTS to confirm findings and determine whether further testing is warranted

desc: 【UTS】Assess septic tanks’ impact on groundwater quality (safely managed sanitation) in Kiritimati
full: Conduct a longitudinal assessment of shallow groundwater quality around newly installed septic tanks to understand potential contamination pathways and impacts. Work completed includes agreement on study design, government buy-in, construction of 40 piezometers across 5 sites (households and one school), installation of rain gauges and water level loggers, and soil sampling; groundwater sampling is scheduled to begin late March/early April. A final report will synthesise findings and implications for safely managed sanitation

desc: 【UTS】Lao PDR technical support package (WSP, GEDSI, LCC, private sector)
full: Deliver a multi-part technical assistance package: (1) develop climate-resilient water safety planning (WSP) guidelines incorporating GEDSI perspectives; (2) assess life-cycle costs of community-managed water services and develop tools/workshops for government use; (3) scope private sector service delivery models and investment/business case through literature review and interviews (e.g., SNV, Gret, Thrive, TerraClear); and (4) provide ongoing GEDSI oversight/support to ensure inclusion is embedded in planning processes. Current progress includes financial analysis reporting shared for review and private sector interviews completed, with final outputs being prepared for sharing.

desc: Briefing note for Senegal Rep on Hydro-diplomacy - please assign to Martina
full:
Le représentant de l’UNICEF Sénégal doit participer à un atelier de haut niveau sur l'Hydrodiplomatie ce vendredi 27 mars, avec la présence de S.E.M. Cheikh Tidiane Dieye, ministre de l’Hydraulique et de l'Assainissement, organisée dans le cadre de la visite à Dakar de Madame Outi Holopainen, Sous-Secrétaire d’Etat du ministère des Affaires Etrangères de Finlande.  Il y aura aussi la participation de l’ambassadrice de Finlande et de l’Ambassadrice de Suisse.
Est-ce qu’il existe des éléments au niveau régional, continental ou siège sur ce que UNICEF fait dans le domaine de l’hydrodiplomatie ?  Ceci nous permettra d’enrichir le briefing du représentant attendu demain (mercredi 25 mars) en fin de journée.

desc: Selection of Food Technologist to Support Pillar 1 of Tanzania's FFA
full:
Support the selection process of the following individual consultancy process:

Food Technologist (Complementary Foods) – National Individual Consultant

Scope of Work:

The purpose of this consultancy is to support the development, validation, and scale-up of affordable, safe, and nutrient-dense complementary food products for children aged 6–23 months in Tanzania. 

Key Tasks

• Conduct landscape analysis and stakeholder engagement by reviewing existing complementary food products, supply chains, and regulatory frameworks, mapping key actors (e.g. manufacturers, laboratories, ingredient suppliers, distributors, government agencies, NGOs and academia institutions), and defining product profiles in consultation with stakeholders. With special focus in prioritized regions: Dar es Salaam, Arusha, Mbeya, Mwanza and Zanzibar.
• Support the validation of the mapping report and database creation of local food producers. 
• Support with key partners product research, development, and validation by formulating and optimizing at least two nutrient-dense complementary food products using locally available ingredients. This includes ensuring adequate nutrient density, protein quality, micronutrient bioavailability (e.g. iron, zinc) energy density, safety, and sensory acceptability; defining processing and packaging parameters (e.g. extrusion, roasting, germination), packaging specifications and critical control points; and overseeing laboratory testing and refinement through accredited labs and refine formulations accordingly. 
• Strengthen food safety, quality assurance, and regulatory compliance systems by supporting the development and implementation of training toolkits (including HACCP, GMP, and SSOP protocols); support building capacity of producers and quality assurance teams through targeted training; developing compliant product labeling in line with national regulations and the International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes; and establishing shelf-life testing protocols as well as market surveillance and monitoring mechanisms.
• Support local producers and SMEs through continuous, hands-on technical assistance to improve production processes of nutritious first foods, ensure food safety and quality standards are met, and facilitate reformulation of existing products to meet national and international standards.
• In collaboration with key partners support the development of business and distribution models by designing costed, sustainable approaches for production and last-mile delivery; identifying risks and mitigation strategies; and exploring market feasibility of the developed products, based on evidence generated.
• Support the development of technical tools and strengthen capacity among governments, producers, SMEs, and partners through training materials, standard operating procedures (SOPs), workshops, webinars, and on-site coaching, promoting long-term sustainability using the training toolkit developed by UNICEF.
• Facilitate knowledge sharing and coordination by establishing and maintaining a network of local farmers, processors, and stakeholders to promote quality assurance, innovation, and continuous learning, while ensuring regular coordination with UNICEF and partners. 
• Support the generation and dissemination of evidence on product acceptability, shelf-life, and market potential, and compile a final report summarizing key findings, achievements, challenges, and recommendations for scale-up.
• Support other activities agreed as relevant to the objectives of the consultancy.

Technical support – Phillipines Solar PV (Tawi-Tawi Project). PCO initially proceeded by engaging a solar PV consultant to provide technical oversight and ensure quality assurance across design, procurement, and installation phases. However, the CO encountered a key constraint ,limited availability of specialized PV expertise in the current market, particularly with experience in complex, remote, and fragile contexts such as Tawi-Tawi. This created delays and required them to rethink their approach as they still do not a PV consultant on board. To further address this, PCO adapted the structure of the engagement and split the institutional ToR into two phases:
1. Technical Assessment and System Design (current phase)
2. Supply, Installation, and Commissioning (implementation phase)
PCO now have SESCO engaged as the technical design firm, and Daniel’s team has been instrumental in supporting the technical review and strengthening the quality of this process.
Looking ahead, PCO are planning a project inception in June, which will bring together relevant BARMM line ministries, and the Languyan local government unit. This will be a critical milestone to align on the technical design, confirm roles and responsibilities, and ensure government ownership from the outset.
Following the completion of this technical phase, PCO will move into the design of  ToR B, which will focus on:
• Procurement and island logistics
• Installation and integration of the hybrid solar PV system
• Commissioning and performance validation
• Capacity building for LGU staff and local youth
• Sustainability and O&M planning

desc: Support for JASW - learning and review
full:
I hope this message finds you both well. As you know, Nigeria continues to be at the forefront of the Joint Action to Stop Wasting (JASW) initiative. Last year’s joint HQ/RO mission was extremely valuable for our team, and we are now moving full steam ahead into the accelerated implementation phase, ensuring we integrate key recommendations from the 2025 mission along the way.

With less than four months remaining until the pilot concludes, we feel it is essential to bring in an external perspective to review our programme implementation and provide technical input for any necessary course corrections. This kind of support will be especially important in helping us capture and document our learnings and country experiences—an integral requirement for JASW. We also look forward to coordinating this effort closely with our WFP colleagues.

In light of this, we kindly request technical support from the CoE for a 3-4 week in-country mission. The specialist would be based in the Kano Field Office and would play a key role in supporting the rollout of our accelerated plan. We would be truly grateful for this additional layer of support as we move towards the final stages of the pilot.

This request is urgent given the end of the main supporting grant for JASW is in September 2026, and we need to complete documentation, in collaboration with WFP, before then.

The team are working on a full ToR.

desc: Technical Assistance for Immunization Supply Chain Digitalization and Strengthening of Vaccine & Cold Chain Management Systems
full:
As part of efforts to strengthen the performance and resilience of the immunization supply chain, the Government, with UNICEF support, is requesting targeted technical assistance from the Centre of Excellence (CoE) – Vaccine & Cold Chain Management to accelerate the digitalization of vaccine and cold chain management.
This request aims to support the country’s transition toward a fully operational eLMIS/eSIGL, ensuring real-time visibility of vaccine stocks, cold chain equipment and temperature monitoring data, in line with WHO–UNICEF guidance and the Gavi 6.0 digitalization strategy.
The requested technical assistance will focus on:
- the development and operationalization of a national roadmap for immunization supply chain digitalization;
- the deployment, integration and country ownership of digital tools, including eLMIS/eSIGL (mSupply), eSMT and temperature monitoring solutions;
- strengthening governance, data quality and system interoperability;
- building national capacities through structured training, on-the-job coaching and institutionalization of digitalized processes.
This support will contribute to reducing stock-outs and vaccine losses, improving data-driven decision-making, and establishing a sustainable, integrated and country-led logistics information system, ultimately ensuring equitable and uninterrupted access to lifesaving vaccines.

desc: Technical Assistance for Vaccine Policy Optimization Planning and Evidence-Based Immunization Decision-Making in Bangladesh
full:
"Bangladesh’s Expanded Programme on Immunization (EPI), in collaboration with partners, is advancing efforts to optimize vaccine policy and program efficiency. A comprehensive Vaccine Policy Optimization Plan (VPOP) is under consideration, with the National Immunization Technical Advisory Group (NITAG) proposing a dedicated retreat with members and partners to guide this process.
Recent developments in the immunization programme include:
• Ongoing discussions on the Hexavalent (Hexa) vaccine switch
• Implementation of changes to the Inactivated Polio Vaccine (IPV) schedule
• Exploration of transitioning from PCV10 to PCV13
• Preliminary discussions on revising the Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine (PCV) schedule from a three-dose to a two-dose regimen
With the increasing share of co-financing (Co-F) by the Government of Bangladesh, there is a strategic need to align vaccine policy decisions with long-term sustainability and programmatic efficiency.
2. Purpose of the Assignment
To provide technical assistance to the national EPI, NITAG, and partners in developing a comprehensive and evidence-based VPOP, ensuring alignment with global best practices and national priorities.
3. Objectives
The COE Technical Expert will:
• Support the design and facilitation of a VPOP retreat with NITAG members and key stakeholders
• Provide technical guidance on vaccine policy decisions, including:
o Hexa vaccine introduction/switch
o IPV schedule optimization
o PCV product switch (PCV10 to PCV13)
o PCV schedule revision (3-dose vs 2-dose)
• Analyze programmatic, epidemiological, and financial implications of proposed changes
• Facilitate consensus-building among EPI, NITAG, and partners
• Strengthen evidence-based decision-making processes"

desc: Technical Assistance for Vaccine Prioritization and Optimization under Gavi 6.0, Including HPV Cecolin Product Switch and MR Vaccine Presentation Transition Pla
full: Technical Assistance Request: Support to Vaccine Prioritization and Optimization for the Gavi 6.0 Strategic Period, with specific focus on the HPV vaccine product switch to Cecolin and the MR vaccine presentation switch from the 10-dose vial to the 5-dose vial. The technical assistance will support assessment, planning, operational readiness, supply chain implications, cost optimization [as it will require tradeoffs within the vaccine envelope], implementation planning, and stakeholder coordination to ensure efficient and sustainable transition within the national immunization program.

desc: Technical Support for CPHA Alliance Annual Meeting Session Development on Integrating, Localizing, and Sustaining Quality Child and Adolescent MHPSS in Humanita
full:
"Technical support to contribute to the development and submission for the Child Protection in Humanitarian Action (CPHA) Alliance Annual Meeting session preparation under the theme Integrating, Localizing and Sustaining Quality MHPSS in Humanitarian Settings, building on the IRCR paper titled From Humanitarianisation to Institutionalization: Exploring the Political Economy of Child and Adolescent Mental Health in Syria, Lebanon, Haiti, and Colombia.

The support will include technical input, review, coordination, and contribution to the framing of key messages and evidence related to the integration, localization, sustainability, and institutionalization of child and adolescent MHPSS within humanitarian and development settings."

desc: Technical Assistance for the Preparation and Delivery of a Neonatal and Child Health Care Workshop Aligned with Updated PHC, Nutrition, Breastfeeding, and Kanga
full:
"Technical assistance is requested to support the preparation and delivery of a workshop on neonatal and child health care, including Primary Health Care staff, in line with commitments agreed with the donor. The workshop will be implemented in coordination with the Maternal and Child Health Programme, using a blended modality (in-person and virtual), and will focus on updating national standards and procedures, including breastfeeding, maternal and lactating women’s nutrition, and Kangaroo Mother Care.
This request is short-term and targeted, intended to ensure technical quality and timely implementation during a transition period, until the new Health and Nutrition Officer is onboarded, while ensuring continuity and compliance with UNICEF technical standards."

desc: Technical Support for Multi-Sectoral MHPSS Coordination, Tool Harmonization, and Standards Alignment in Afghanistan
full:
"Afghanistan faces a severe MHPSS crisis affecting children and caregivers, with high levels of psychological distress driven by legacy of years of conflict, poverty and restrictions on women and girls. UNICEF is planning to implement a multi-sectoral MHPSS model; however, service delivery remains fragmented with limited harmonization of tools and weak coordination.
Technical support is requested to facilitate structured consultations to (a) clarify multi-sectoral coordination across CP, Health, Education and SBC; (b) define practical implementation approaches; (c) harmonize evidence-based tools; and (d) ensure alignment with UNICEF, WHO and Inter-Agency MHPSS standards."

desc: Technical Support for Adaptation of Evidence-Based MHPSS Interventions for Child-, School-, and Community-Based Service Delivery Platforms in Afghanistan
full:
"Adapt existing age- and gender-appropriate evidence-based MHPSS interventions for use in various services delivery point:
(a) Child-Friendly Spaces (CFS)
(b) Women and Girls Safe Spaces (WGSS)
(b) Multi-purpose centres (MPCs)
(c) Schools and community-based education platforms
Note- Adaptation should ensure:
Cultural relevance (Afghanistan context), addressing barriers to inclusion of adolescent girls and vulnerable groups"

desc: Technical Support for Adaptation of Evidence-Based Materials and Development of Standardized Cross-Sectoral Training and Mentoring Packages for Frontline Workfo

Should include:
Outcome-level indicators
Measurement tools (e.g., SDQ, resilience, well-being scales)
Data collection and analysis guidance
Integration with existing systems (e.g., sector reporting)"

desc: Pilot of the Public Procurement Efficiency priogramme in Sierra Leone within the Education Sector
full:
Work with the Ministry of Basic and Senor Secondary Education, the Ministry of Technical and Higher Education and Ministry of Finance on the following Steps, Key Activities and Analysis:
1.  Develop a Value for Money Agenda Agree with MBSSE/MTHE and MoF on priority commodities and processes to target (e.g. textbooks, furniture, school supplies). Review existing policies; set monitoring indicators and implementation structures; produce a workplan and agreed terms of reference.
2.  Identify Inefficiencies Full diagnostic of the procurement cycle: map actors, resource flows, rules and decision points. Identify bottlenecks – misaligned budget and procurement timelines, fragmented systems, weak specifications, payment arrears, limited competition, etc. Produce a prioritised action plan.
3.  Monitor and Measure Savings Assess existing M&E and IFMIS capacity to track savings at each procurement stage. Strengthen data collection and reporting. Institutionalise routine VfM analysis – comparing contracted prices to benchmarks, tracking budget execution against procurement plans.
4.  Protect and Reinvest via PFM Diagnose the PFM rules governing what happens to savings (annuality, rollover, common pooling rules). Identify options ranging from in-year reprioritisation within MBSSE/MTHE to creation of protected budget lines and MTEF embedding. Advocate for legal or administrative changes where needed.

desc: Charles Knox Vydmanov, Social Protection Specialist from COE are requested to provide mission in Mongolia for Reform agenda on PwDs and CwDs SP.
full:
The specialist will provide technical assistance to UNICEF Mongolia and Government counterparts through consultations, technical workshops, field engagement and operational planning activities to support implementation of ongoing social protection reforms.
Key areas of support include:
1. Reform Acceleration and Technical Advice
Provide technical advice on gender-responsive, disability-inclusive and needs-based social protection reforms, including service delivery systems, disability inclusion, service coordination mechanisms and social welfare reform priorities.
2. Reform Scoping and Activity Prioritization
Support development of detailed implementation pathways, activity prioritization and sequencing for GRSP II activities and Government reform priorities, including identification of operational bottlenecks and technical support needs.
3. Capacity Strengthening and Knowledge Exchange
Facilitate technical workshop, practical training session and stakeholder consultation with core stakeholders to strengthen understanding and implementation capacities related to inclusive social protection systems.
4. Data Systems and Service Integration
Provide technical inputs on strengthening social protection information systems, data exchange mechanisms, interoperability opportunities and service integration approaches within existing platforms and institutional arrangements.
5. Partnership and Stakeholder Engagement
Facilitate engagement with Government institutions, development partners, organizations of persons with disabilities and other stakeholders to strengthen coordination and implementation readiness.

full: Support for the development of standardized tools for WASH damage assessments to improve data quality, consistency, and timely decision-making.

desc: Case study on R-WASH
full: Technical Assistance on developing a case study for works that are on going now under R WASH

desc: Decentralized Waste Water  Systems in Humanitarian Settings
full: Technical support is required to explore and operationalize innovative decentralized waste water systems for neighbourhood in cities  and in displacement affected and high density areas,  financing mechanisms, including cost recovery models, subsidies, and market-based approaches to improve sustainability of waste water decentrlized services.

desc: Comunal latrines in Markets, mosques and public areas with charges
full: Technical assistance is required to establish a sustainable management framework for communal latrines in public spaces, focusing on the design of cost-recovery models, the training of local sanitation entrepreneurs, and the installation of durable, odor-free technologies like bio-digesters and water-seal units.

desc: Design for desalination Plants in RED SEA State under AFDB
full: Technical  Assisstance in developing design for desalination projects in Red Sea under AFDB

desc: Developing guidelines to overcome governance issues
full: Developing guidelines to overcome governance challenges in the WASH sector, including limited institutional coordination, unclear roles and responsibilities, and weak accountability mechanisms, continue to affect the efficiency, sustainability, and equitable delivery of services.

desc: Conflict-resilient water sources
full: Request for technical assistance to assess, design, and standardize conflict-resilient water supply systems, including development of adapted designs, BOQs, and O&M frameworks to ensure continuous and reliable water access in insecure and hard-to-reach areas.

desc: Evidence based investment
full: Development of an evidence-based investment case to support the rehabilitation and strengthening of WASH services in damaged and underserved areas, ensuring sustainable, equitable, and resilient service delivery.

desc: Develop a WASHcurriculum for integration into school manuals:
full: A technical assistant is required to integrate into school curriculum

desc: GIS mapping for the water netwroks in Big cities
full: Request for technical assistance to develop and operationalize GIS-based mapping systems for urban water networks, enabling improved planning, monitoring, and management of WASH services in large cities.

desc: Integration of WASH with key sectors
full: Strengthening the integration of WASH with key sectors—including health, education, nutrition, and urban development—to enhance service delivery, improve public health outcomes, and promote sustainable and inclusive community resilience.

desc: House hold filters local  manfacturing
full: Request for Technical assisstance  to upgrade Gadarif's existing community-based filtration methods by standardizing the local manufacturing of low-cost biosand and ceramic designs to ensure scalable, sustainable, and high-quality household water treatment.

desc: Local sanitation marketing
full: Request for technical assistance to design and implement a local sanitation marketing approach, strengthening demand, supply chains, and access to affordable and sustainable sanitation products and services.

desc: Documentation of solar-powered borehole systems
full: Documentation of solar-powered borehole systems, including design specifications, installation processes, and operational performance, to ensure standardization, quality assurance, and sustainable operation of water supply systems.

desc: Faculty of engineering and Urban WASH integration
full: Development of an integrated capacity-building programme linking the Faculty of Engineering with Urban WASH systems to strengthen technical skills, promote applied learning, and support sustainable and resilient urban service delivery.

desc: Establish tracking system for the WASH cose pipline
full: Request for technical assistance to design and implement a tracking system for the WASH core pipeline, enabling real-time visibility, efficient stock management, and improved coordination of supply distribution.

desc: Documentation of water treatment plants
full: Documentation of water treatment plants, including design specifications, operational processes, and performance monitoring, to ensure quality assurance, standardization, and sustainable water service delivery.

desc: Flood adabted latrines
full: flood-adapted sanitation design, site assessment, standardized BOQs, safe sludge management, and rapid deployment models, supported by capacity building and O&M systems.

desc: Plantation and encouraging green to reduce heat
full: Integrating greenery into schools and health centers transforms these institutions into "cooling sanctuaries" that protect vulnerable students and patients from Sudan's extreme heat while naturally purifying the air. By prioritizing native, drought-resistant trees, these facilities can lower indoor temperatures significantly, creating a more focused learning environment for children and a more restful, healing atmosphere for recovery.

desc: Piloting WASH cash and vouchers approach to supplies
full: Technical assistance is required to design and pilot water voucher systems for IDP gathering sites as a market-based approach to improve access, efficiency, and accountability in emergency water supply.

desc: Introducing water metering systems in big cities across Sudan
full: Technical assistance is needed l to establish a comprehensive household metering framework where none currently exists, focusing on the design of localized installation protocols, the development of a consumer database, and the training of technical teams to manage the transition from flat-rate billing to transparent, consumption-based revenue collection.

desc: Quantifcation of Remaining works for Gadarif Water supply system
full: To provide the technical support required to quantify the remaining works for the Gadarif water infrastructure, specifically the connection between the city and the Upper Atbara and Setit Dam Complex, to conduct a structured Gap Analysis and Technical Asset Verification. Gadarif has historical water problems and shortages

desc: Rocky Soil islands latriens designs
full: Request for technical assistance to design appropriate latrine and septic tank systems for rocky soil conditions in island settings, including development of standardized designs and BOQs to ensure safe, durable, and context-adapted sanitation solutions.

desc: Qauntifying remaining works for Big WTPs in Gazeira, Abuhamd and Shendi
full: Technical Assisstance to quanitfy electromechanical works in completed big Water treatemnts plants of capacities ranginfg from 30,000 to 75,0000 m3/h in Gazeira, Shendi Abuhamad and Kosti

desc: Predictive Maintaince for Boreholes in Rural areas
full: Request of Technical Assisstance to  use predictive maitiance of borehols by creating and installation of sensors (Digital Twin Monitoring) Real-time sensors  of rural borehole networks. This allows technicians to see a drop in pressure before a pump actually breaks, enabling predictive maintenance rather than reactive repairs. can you elaborate more

desc: Review and Strengthening of Capacity Building and Operational Manuals for Local Partners
full: Technical assistance is required to review existing training materials and operational manuals for WASH implementing partners to ensure they reflect current humanitarian standards, contextual realities, and practical field application.

desc: local production of emergency latrine slabs
full: Request for technical assistance to establish and scale local production of emergency latrine slabs, including standardized designs, quality assurance, and supply chain development to support rapid and cost-effective sanitation response

desc: Managed Aquifer Recharge in East Sudan
full: Technical support is required to conceptualize and operationalize Managed Aquifer Recharge (MAR) suitability mapping in Eastern Sudan under the R-WASH funded project, where groundwater depletion is accelerating due to intensive irrigation practices.

desc: Sanitation Financing in Humanitarian Settings
full: Technical support is required to explore and operationalize innovative sanitation financing mechanisms, including cost recovery models, subsidies, and market-based approaches to improve sustainability of sanitation services.

desc: Small Town Water Supply Approaches in Protracted Displacement Contexts
full: flood-adapted sanitation design, site assessment, standardized BOQs, safe sludge management, and rapid deployment models, supported by capacity building and O&M systems.

desc: Sanitary pads local production
full: Provision of technical assistance to establish and strengthen local production of sanitary pads, ensuring quality, affordability, and sustainable access to menstrual hygiene products.

desc: Satellit -Aided Predictive Drilling
full: Seeking technical assistance to implement satellite-aided predictive drilling in Sudan, utilizing remote sensing and advanced hydrogeological modeling to identify high-yield, climate-resilient aquifers and minimize the high rate of unsuccessful borehole construction in the Gadarif region

desc: Sanitation and Faecal Sludge Management (FSM)
full: Technical assistance is required to strengthen emergency and protracted sanitation systems, including desludging services, safe faecal sludge collection, transport, treatment, and disposal in both IDP settlements and urban settings.

desc: Solid Waste Management in Humanitarian Contexts
full: Support is needed to design and implement effective solid waste management systems, including waste collection, segregation, transportation, and environmentally safe disposal in displacement-affected and high-density areas.

desc: Study on Heavy metals contamination in drinking water
full: Technical assisstance is needed to study heavy metals contamination in River Nile where heavy mining activies are taking place and its effect on ground water and river water

desc: Small-Scale Income Generating Activities Linked to WASH Programming
full: Support is needed to design and implement livelihood-linked WASH initiatives such as soap production, fuel briquette making, and latrine slab fabrication to strengthen hygiene practices, improve sanitation access, and support household income generation.

desc: Technical Support on cross border implemntation laisons between UNICEF offices
full: Procurement services to establish contractors dta base for cross border works

desc: Technology in networks capacity building
full: Request for technical assistance to build capacity in the use of robotics and advanced technologies for leak detection and repair in water distribution networks, enhancing efficiency, reducing water losses, and improving system performance.

desc: video documentation of WASH interventions
full: Production of end-to-end video documentation capturing the full WASH service chain from water source to distribution and point of use to support quality assurance, monitoring, and knowledge sharing.

desc: Specailized contractors data base and suppliers
full: Technical Assisstance is needed to list and categorize contractors and suppliers by specailty, presence and financial capacity

desc: WASH Cluster Information Management Strengthening
full: Technical support is required to reinforce coordination at national and sub-national levels, including partner mapping (4Ws), data analysis, gap identification, dashboard development, and timely reporting to support evidence-based decision-making.

desc: WASH FIT
full: Technical Assistance is needed to design the tool for WASH fit Assessment in Sudan

desc: Technical Review of Emergency WASH Interventions in Context
full: Support is needed to conduct technical reviews of ongoing emergency WASH interventions to ensure appropriateness, quality, cost-effectiveness, and alignment with humanitarian standards and local context.

desc: Water turbin as renewable energy source
full: Support is needed to study and explore sustainable and cost-effective energy solutions to support water supply systems, particularly in areas with access to continuous surface water sources such as the Nile and irrigation canals.

desc: Training on commity on financial literacy for Water
full: Technical assisstance on providing trainings to the community. Community  is trained not just in repair, but in financial literacy for water. They pay a tiny monthly subscription  that covers the insurance premium, ensuring that if a major component breaks, the funds for a replacement are available immediately without needing a donor.

desc: Wind Turbine as renewable enrgy source for water supply in Red Sea and Northern States
full: Expertise support is required to assess the feasibility of utilizing wind energy (wind turbines) for power generation to supply water treatment and desalination plants in the Red Sea region and northern states. The assessment should explore technical, economic, and operational viability, including the potential for integrating hybrid renewable energy systems.

desc: Menstrual health and hygiene national political economy analysis, advocacy and system strengthening support
full: Meet with Tanzania delegation at the African Menstrual Product Standard & Market Development Forum and discuss MHH programming including national PEA, advocacy plans and system strengthening support, aligned with ASWA and Osprey/Waterloo partnerships - planning and implementation support.

full: Support to CO on Global level Youth engagement at the Dushanbe Water conference and connecting it to the UN Water Conference 2026

desc: Assessment of water user committee sustainability for rural water O&M in Timor-Leste
full: We would like to request regional PCA partners (assuming University Tech Sydney based on their previous support to TL) to consider new assessment and analysis work in Timor-Leste regarding sustainability of community-run water user committees in terms of rural water system operation and maintenance.
